raw_title: Data Practices and Privacy in Gaming: A Consolidated Cross‐Platform Analysis of Publishing Platforms, Online Multiplayer Games and Community Sites
raw_abstract: ABSTRACT Video games are mainstream cultural artefacts that millions engage with every day, constituting an increasingly data‐intensive aspect of people's leisure time. Previous research has established the foundational role data plays in the production and distribution of video games, as well as the management of gamers as platform users and advertising audiences. This study consolidates this view and establishes a baseline of data practices in gaming. Addressing a broad audience of privacy researchers and policy stakeholders, the aim of the study is to provide a comparative analysis of gaming data privacy against other digital platform domains and social media use cases. To this end, the study presents the results of a qualitative analysis that investigated 37 cases of online multiplayer games, video game publishing platforms and online community sites. By analysing their privacy policies, the study sketches the common data practices in the gaming context and lays the foundations for future systematic research on user privacy, trust and literacy amongst gamers and enables cross‐disciplinary engagement with gaming and the gaming data subject position.
title_en: Data Practices and Privacy in Gaming: A Consolidated Cross‐Platform Analysis of Publishing Platforms, Online Multiplayer Games and Community Sites
title_zh: 游戏中的数据实践与隐私：对发布平台、在线多人游戏和社区网站的综合跨平台分析
abstract_en: ABSTRACT Video games are mainstream cultural artefacts that millions engage with every day, constituting an increasingly data‐intensive aspect of people's leisure time. Previous research has established the foundational role data plays in the production and distribution of video games, as well as the management of gamers as platform users and advertising audiences. This study consolidates this view and establishes a baseline of data practices in gaming. Addressing a broad audience of privacy researchers and policy stakeholders, the aim of the study is to provide a comparative analysis of gaming data privacy against other digital platform domains and social media use cases. To this end, the study presents the results of a qualitative analysis that investigated 37 cases of online multiplayer games, video game publishing platforms and online community sites. By analysing their privacy policies, the study sketches the common data practices in the gaming context and lays the foundations for future systematic research on user privacy, trust and literacy amongst gamers and enables cross‐disciplinary engagement with gaming and the gaming data subject position.
abstract_zh: 摘要 电子游戏是主流文化产品，每天有数百万人参与，构成了人们休闲时间中日益数据密集的方面。先前的研究已经确立了数据在电子游戏生产和发行中的基础作用，以及将游戏玩家作为平台用户和广告受众进行管理的作用。本研究巩固了这一观点，并建立了游戏数据实践的基线。针对隐私研究人员和政策利益相关者的广泛受众，本研究旨在提供游戏数据隐私与其他数字平台领域和社交媒体用例的比较分析。为此，本研究展示了对37个在线多人游戏、视频游戏发布平台和在线社区网站的定性分析结果。通过分析它们的隐私政策，本研究勾勒了游戏环境中的常见数据实践，并为未来关于游戏玩家用户隐私、信任和素养的系统研究奠定了基础，并促进了与游戏和游戏数据主体地位的跨学科参与。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究调查了游戏中的数据隐私问题，分析了37个在线游戏、发布平台和社区网站。研究发现，游戏收集大量用户数据，但隐私政策往往复杂。研究旨在帮助人们了解游戏数据隐私与其他数字平台（如社交媒体）的差异，为未来保护玩家隐私的研究打下基础。
plain_summary_en: This study looks at data privacy in gaming by analyzing 37 online games, publishing platforms, and community sites. It finds that games collect a lot of user data, but privacy policies can be confusing. The research aims to show how gaming data privacy compares to other digital platforms like social media, and sets the stage for future work to better protect players' privacy.
research_direction_zh: 探索游戏行业中的数据收集、使用和隐私保护实践，比较不同平台间的差异，并为制定相关政策和提高用户隐私素养提供依据。
research_direction_en: Investigating data collection, usage, and privacy practices in the gaming industry, comparing differences across platforms, and informing policy development and user privacy literacy.
